topic: Why intelligent people believe conspiracy theories

script.title: Why Smart People Fall for Conspiracies

scenes.narration: The smartest person you know might believe something completely insane. And research shows their intelligence is exactly why they cannot see it.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, a single chess king toppled on a cracked mirror, sharp shadows, high contrast, no people, no faces, cinematic

scenes.narration: Psychologists call it motivated reasoning. The brain does not search for truth. It searches for arguments that protect what you already believe.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, a magnifying glass hovering over a torn page with redacted lines, dust particles, deep shadows, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Higher intelligence makes this worse, not better. Smarter people build more convincing rationalizations for the very things they should question.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, intricate wooden puzzle pieces locking into a cracked wall, dim spotlight, smoke, no people, no faces, cinematic

scenes.narration: Then there is the pattern recognition trap. Your brain is wired to see connections, even where none exist. The more clever you are, the better the false patterns look.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, red string connecting dozens of pinned papers on a dark wall, single bare bulb, deep shadows, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Conspiracy thinking also offers something rare. A sense of being chosen. Of seeing what the sleeping masses cannot. That feeling is intoxicating.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, a single open eye carved into a stone tablet surrounded by closed eye carvings, dramatic side lighting, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Stress fuels the fire. Studies show that when humans feel powerless, they hunt for hidden enemies. Order becomes a story about secret control.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, marionette strings descending into darkness above a small wooden chair, harsh contrast, smoke, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Once a belief becomes part of your identity, contradicting evidence feels like a personal attack. Smart people just defend it harder.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, cracked iron mask resting on an old book, candlelight, deep shadows, no people, no faces, cinematic

scenes.narration: So when you meet someone brilliant who believes something baffling, do not assume they are stupid. Their mind is doing exactly what yours would do under the right pressure.
scenes.image_prompt: black and white film noir, a single old key suspended by a thin chain in front of a heavy locked door, mist, dramatic shadows, no people, no faces
